title: salix nigricans subsp. borealis
intro: Salix nigricans subsp. borealis, also known as the Black Willow, is a subspecies of the Salix nigricans plant, a type of willow tree native to boreal regions. The Black Willow has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, derived from its bark and leaves. The health benefits of Salix nigricans subsp. borealis include relief from pain, fever reduction, and the treatment of various skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the Black Willow include teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
